summary: velocityfix ships Windows PE dropper that exfiltrates host data to 751.lol at npm install
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall hook ("node scripts/loader.js") spawns cmd.exe to execute a bundled Windows binary, payload.exe, against the installer's machine. The payload contains a WinHTTP multipart/form-data uploader that POSTs host data (username, os_info, ip_address, and a data.zip archive) to https://751.lol/upload/, reads Windows ProductName/DisplayVersion from the registry, and performs anti-analysis checks for sandbox and VM artifacts (SbieDll.dll, snxhk.dll, Vmmouse.sys, Vboxguest.sys, VMware/VBox drivers, GPU ProviderName). Package metadata (name 'velocityfix', description 'Fix for velocity executor', keywords minecraft/velocity/proxy, author 'Velocity Team') impersonates the legitimate PaperMC Velocity Minecraft proxy to lure installers, but the package contains no Minecraft/proxy code — only the loader and the stealer binary. Default `npm install` on Windows runs the dropper automatically, providing the attacker with stolen host data and a foothold on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
